flashcards.back: A path that traverses every edge exactly once in a graph. Vertices may be repeated.
A path that visits each vertex exactly once in a graph. Edges may not all be used.

flashcards.front: What condition must be satisfied for an undirected graph to have an Eulerian circuit?
flashcards.back: All vertices must have even degree and the graph must be connected.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of finding a Hamiltonian path?
flashcards.back: NP-complete - no known polynomial time algorithm exists for general graphs.

flashcards.front: What famous problem led to the development of Eulerian path theory?
flashcards.back: The Königsberg Bridge Problem solved by Leonhard Euler in 1736.
